paper_map.title: Deciphering the composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels with machine learning
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study employs machine learning classification and regression models to establish the composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels. The models predict four key phase characteristics: existence of the κ phase, presence of a (γ+κ) duplex region at aging temperature, a single γ region, and κ phase volume fraction. Al and C are identified as dominant influencing elements, with predicted composition ranges for desired phase regions. Experimental validation on Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C steel shows excellent agreement with predictions, demonstrating a powerful design roadmap for low-density steels.
paper_map.research_objective: Elucidate the correlation between the complex composition and resulting microstructure in low-density FeMnAlC austenitic steels by using machine learning to accelerate alloy design.
material_systems: Nominal composition range: 18-30 wt% Mn, 8-12 wt% Al, 0.6-1.8 wt% C
Key phases: γ austenite, κ-carbide (κ phase), α-ferrite, β-Mn, B2/DO3 intermetallics
main_process_variables: Data descriptors for machine learning (composition features)
sample_aliases: Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C steel (nominal) with measured composition Fe-30.4Mn-7.9Al-1.1C (wt%)
